title: Task
description:
A Task is the atomic reusable unit of business activity: a Role performing exactly one Operation. A Task reads as '<Role> does <Operation>' (e.g. 'Underwriter does Loan.Approve'). Different Operation = different Task. Tasks are referenced by Steps inside a Process and are the primary unit of composition for SOPs.

The `actor` field references a Role (declared in the domain's roles[]). System actors are Roles with `system: true`.

name.description: The kebab-case stable identifier for this task.

description.description: A human-readable explanation of what this task accomplishes and why.

actor.description: The Role that performs this task. References a declared Role (human or system).

operation.description: The Operation (Target.Action) this task invokes. Must reference a declared Operation. One Task = one Operation.

domain.description: The dot-separated domain path this task is scoped to.

examples.name: close-loan
examples.description: Closing specialist closes an approved loan, preparing it for disbursement.
